rationale: Trump calling off the third night of Iran strikes is a qualitative development — potential de-escalation reduces near-term energy price risk for Poland, but the conflict remains active and unresolved, keeping indirect impact elevated.

top_events.title: Trump halts third night of Iran strikes after Kharg Island threat
top_events.summary: After two days of mutual US-Iran strikes, Trump called off a third night of attacks, having previously threatened Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal. This potential pause reduces the immediate risk of a major oil supply shock, which would directly affect fuel and energy prices in Poland.

top_events.title: World Bank: US-Iran war to drag global economy to post-COVID low
top_events.summary: The World Bank warns that the ongoing US-Iran conflict risks pushing the global economy to its weakest point since COVID-19. This has real indirect consequences for Poland's export-dependent economy and employment levels.

top_events.title: Powerful earthquake kills at least 37 in southern Philippines
top_events.summary: A strong earthquake struck the southern Philippines, killing at least 37 people. Geographically distant from Poland with no meaningful impact on Polish daily life.
